description_long: This is a rectangular stone tomb or sarcophagus, set on a slightly raised stone platform with a beveled edge around the lid. The structure is made of weathered gray stone, with visible cracks and some areas of moss and ivy growth, particularly at the base and corners. The sides feature recessed panels bordered by simple molding, and the corners are adorned with decorative, urn-like supports. The top surface is flat and broad, suitable for placing objects or flowers, and the entire monument sits on a paved stone base that extends outward, forming a small step. The overall appearance suggests age and exposure to the elements, with discoloration and plant life adding to its historic character. No inscriptions or engravings are visible on the panels.
description: A weathered rectangular stone tomb with decorative corners, set on a raised stone platform and partially covered in moss and ivy.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main recessed panel, decorative corner supports, and ivy creeping up the left side of the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow end, decorative molding, and some moss at the base.
description_view_2: Rear view with a large recessed panel, significant ivy growth on the right, and visible weathering on the stone.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the plain end, decorative supports, and more scattered ivy at the base.
